headline: Jamaican Police Officer's Mentorship Program Reaches 10,000 Youth
meta_description: A Jamaican police officer from an inner-city community created a youth mentorship program now serving over 10,000 young people, earning Commonwealth recognitio…
summary_short: A police officer from Jamaica's inner-city neighborhoods built a mentorship program that has transformed the lives of more than 10,000 young people.
body:
A police officer who grew up in one of Jamaica's inner-city communities has created a youth mentorship program that now reaches more than 10,000 young people. Drawing on her own lived experience, she designed an initiative to provide guidance, support, and opportunities to young people facing similar circumstances to those she navigated growing up.

The program has become a beacon of hope in the communities it serves, offering mentorship that extends beyond policing into genuine personal development and life skills. Her work has been recognized with a Commonwealth Caribbean award, acknowledging the profound impact of her dedication and vision. The scale of the program—now touching the lives of tens of thousands of young people—demonstrates both her determination and the deep need for mentorship in these communities.

Her journey from inner-city youth to community leader shows how lived experience and commitment can transform lives at scale.
